findings.claim: Shared-key ownership ('does THIS surface render+bind THIS shared key?') is now centralized behind one internal pure non-reactive helper partition_view (R/paintr-partition-view.R, ADR 0026 rank 11) — LANDED in b6a2f72, no longer a parked proposal: three surface roles (ptr_partition_roles = c('single','panel_host','instance')) — 'single' (single-instance L1, owns all keys + renders its inline shared section), 'panel_host' (L2 standalone panel, owns the >=2-formula panel keys), 'instance' (embedded ptr_server, defers panel keys to the host) — each construct a view via new_partition_view(role, panel_keys, consumer_keys) and query partition_host_owned_keys()/partition_renders_section(), replacing the three previously hand-derived host_owned_keys complement sets + the render_shared_section boolean; the partition ALGORITHM (shared_partition in paintr-shared-coordinator.R) is unchanged — only the consumption side was unified.
findings.detail: This is the channel where the recurring 2026-05-22 partition-binder bug lived. Parked (ADR 0026) as a design abstraction for an interactive session, not landed.
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7 against R/paintr-partition-view.R (new_partition_view/partition_host_owned_keys/partition_renders_section; ptr_partition_roles=c('single','panel_host','instance')) and the four call sites: R/paintr-app.R:169 (partition_renders_section single), :339 (single complement = character(0)), :1166-1167 (instance, panel_keys=names(dots$shared)); R/paintr-shared-ui.R:416-418 (panel_host, setdiff(consumer_keys,panel_keys)). shared_partition algorithm unchanged (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:28-49). The prior claim's 'C1/partition-view candidate proposes one internal partition_view ... not landed' is now FALSE — it landed in b6a2f72 (ADR 0026 #11); claim rewritten to current truth. Note actual signature is new_partition_view(role, panel_keys, consumer_keys), not the proposed partition_view(part, surface_id).

findings.claim: ggpaintr has TWO deliberately separate R-name normalizers: the column-vector normalizer (ptr_normalize_column_name_vector — allows dots, pads/truncates to length n, make.unique across columns) and the single binding-name normalizer (ptr_make_valid_name — collapse non-[alnum_] to _, trim, fallback-if-empty, reserved-word suffix, make.names).
findings.detail: ptr_make_valid_name now backs BOTH the file-derived default name (ptr_upload_default_name = thin wrapper) and the typed-shortcut name (the else branch in ptr_resolve_upload_info), killing the prior drift between those two binding-name paths. The column-vector normalizer is intentionally NOT folded in: distinct job (dot-bearing names like Sepal.Length, cross-column make.unique, length padding) whose outputs are pinned by many tests; the upload-default-name path has its own exact-output tests (test-upload.R / test-coverage-gaps.R, e.g. "my-data.csv"->"my_data", "if.csv"->"if_").
findings.verification: Confirmed two distinct normalizers: ptr_normalize_column_name_vector (data.R:77-107; allows dots, pads/truncates to n, make.unique) vs ptr_make_valid_name (upload.R:15-27; no dots, trim, fallback, reserved-word suffix, make.names).

findings.claim: The implementable pre-commit hook blocks any commit touching files matching `dev/plans/*.html | dev/plans/*.md | *-plan.md` unless they carry a current `implementable: PASS` stamp — and the `*-plan.md` glob matches files ANYWHERE, e.g. tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md, not just under dev/plans/.
findings.detail: ADRs under dev/adr/ are NOT gated. Bit this session: a cosmetic edit to tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md blocked the commit; fix was `git restore --source=HEAD --staged --worktree` that file (a plain `git checkout --` left it staged). Avoid editing any *-plan.md during refactors.
findings.source: dev/githooks/pre-commit; observed: editing tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md blocked a commit
findings.verification: Confirmed dev/githooks/pre-commit line 26 case-globs 'dev/plans/*.html|dev/plans/*.md|*-plan.md' requiring a current 'implementable: PASS' stamp (content-hash matched); the *-plan.md glob matches anywhere, not only under dev/plans/.

findings.claim: "x is a usable R binding symbol" is now the single predicate is_syntactic_name(x) = is.character(x) && length(x)==1L && !is.na(x) && nzchar(x) && make.names(x)==x; it was open-coded ~13 times across the source-binding helpers and bound-name cleanup.
findings.detail: Reserved words fail because make.names appends a dot (make.names("if")=="if." != "if"). The !is.na guard only affects NA input, which no call site supplies. as.character(symbol) is always a length-1 non-NA character so the partial-form sites (nzchar(cand) && make.names(cand)==cand) are equivalent under is_syntactic_name.
findings.verification: Re-verified by symbol at HEAD a58dadd. The only R/ behavioral drift since this finding's last verification is consumer_upstream_source_state's host-scope identity (ADR 0026 host env-rename clear); that hunk does not touch this finding's cited symbol(s), which are present and unchanged at HEAD. Claim re-confirmed.

findings.claim: In ptr_setup_pipelines the two panel-owned source observer blocks were identical EXCEPT the bound_names key — the bare-data-layer branch binds under the LAYER name, the pipeline-head branch binds under the SOURCE id — so the extracted wire_panel_owned_source(state, key, node, entry, session, slot) takes `key` explicitly to preserve that duality.
findings.detail: Corroborates the existing bound-names-key-duality understanding. is_panel_owned_id(panel_sources, id) = !is.null(id) && id %in% names(panel_sources %||% list()) replaced ~9 open-coded membership tests; it takes the panel_sources list directly so both state$panel_sources and a local panel_sources call sites share it.
findings.verification: Re-verified by symbol at HEAD a58dadd. The only R/ behavioral drift since this finding's last verification is consumer_upstream_source_state's host-scope identity (ADR 0026 host env-rename clear); that hunk does not touch this finding's cited symbol(s), which are present and unchanged at HEAD. Claim re-confirmed.

findings.claim: The parallel full-suite gate (TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE TESTTHAT_CPUS=4) reproducibly fails ~6 assertions in the consumer-seeding / derived-column browser cluster that PASS when those same files are re-run serially — boot-tail races, not product failures; the per-commit landing gate should be run serially (devtools::test(), ~PASS 3439, 0/0/0).
findings.detail: Corroborates the shinytest2-boot-tail-race understanding. Baseline this session: parallel PASS 3433 with 6 such failures; serial re-run of those 4 files FAIL 0 / PASS 15.
findings.source: TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE devtools::test() vs serial; test-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-consumer-deselect-stays-empty.R, test-default-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R
findings.verification: Observational parallel boot-tail flake finding. Cited test file(s) unchanged at HEAD a58dadd and CLAUDE.md authoritative-gate block still documents this parallel-vs-serial flake behavior. Claim re-confirmed.

findings.claim: The renderUI-container output id for ANY placeholder (value, source, or consumer) is `paste0(raw_id, "_ui")`, now defined exactly once as `placeholder_output_id()` in R/paintr-ids.R.
findings.detail: Previously three byte-identical helpers — value_output_id/source_output_id (paintr-build-ui.R) and consumer_output_id (paintr-ids.R). The widget binds at the raw id; its renderUI container is `<raw_id>_ui` to avoid an inputId/container collision. emit_placeholder_rows()'s former 3-branch role dispatch collapsed to one call since all roles produced the same id.
findings.verification: Confirmed placeholder_output_id<-function(raw_id) paste0(raw_id,'_ui') defined once (ids.R:139-141); callers use it (build-ui.R:63-85, server.R:2177/2252/2317/2675/2988, shared-ui.R:553/905, input-spec.R:339).

findings.claim: ptr_app and ptr_app_grid are ALREADY thin wrappers — each just calls its distinct *_components builder then shiny::shinyApp(ui=parts$ui, server=parts$server); the only shared line is the shinyApp call, so there is no real L1-assembly duplication to extract (deep-leave).
findings.detail: ptr_app_components (single formula -> ptr_translate -> ptr_build_app_ui + ptr_make_app_server) and ptr_app_grid_components (multi-plot grid) are genuinely different assembly, not copies.
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7. The sole intervening diff to the cited file(s) since this finding's baseline is b6a2f72 (the behavior-preserving partition_view seam, ADR 0026 #11), read in full: it only replaced three host_owned_keys derivations + one render_shared_section literal with equivalent partition_view accessor calls and added R/paintr-partition-view.R. It does not intersect this claim's code path; cited symbols and behavior confirmed unchanged. (Line numbers in R/paintr-app.R after ~1168 shifted +4 and in R/paintr-shared-ui.R after ~416 shifted +3; verify by symbol.)

findings.claim: The five exported argument-validator factories were hard-renamed ptr_default_string/_numeric/_numeric_vector/_symbol_or_string/_expression -> ptr_arg_string/_numeric/_numeric_vector/_symbol_or_string/_expression (no deprecated aliases), and the roxygen doc group @name/@rdname ptr_default_args -> ptr_arg_validators.
findings.detail: They return a validator closure function(arg_expr)->canonical|abort for a keyword's formula-time argument, NOT a coercer/default value — hence "arg" not "default". Used at the call site as `positional_arg = ptr_arg_numeric()`. man/ptr_default_args.Rd removed, ptr_arg_validators.Rd added by devtools::document().
findings.verification: Confirmed five ptr_arg_* factories (default-args.R:201-268), NAMESPACE exports ptr_arg_* (90-94) and NO ptr_default_*; no old definitions remain (hard rename); roxygen @name/@rdname ptr_arg_validators.

findings.claim: The three public placeholder constructors (ptr_define_placeholder_value/_consumer/_source) are now thin wrappers over one internal `ptr_define_core(role, ...)` that owns the shared ~20-line validate-then-register prologue; role-specific bits (build_ui arity, resolve hook, validate_input/shortcut acceptance, runtime default, data_aware) branch on `role`.
findings.detail: build_ui validation args = c("node","cols","data") for consumer else c("node"); source validates resolve_data + defaults resolve_expr to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value) + validates the `shortcut` logical; value/consumer validate resolve_expr and accept validate_input; runtime default is identity for value/consumer and an abort-guard ("only meaningful inside ptr_app()") for source.
findings.verification: Confirmed ptr_define_core(role,...) (registry.R:352) owns ensure_registry_initialized/validate_keyword/role-branched hook validation/validate_*/entry build/ptr_registry_register; the three public ptr_define_placeholder_value/_consumer/_source (592/713/870) delegate to it.

findings.claim: After the rename, the placeholder public ARGUMENT names are `positional_arg` / `ui_text_defaults` but the registry-RECORD storage keys stay `entry$default_arg` / `entry$copy_defaults` — the public arg name and the internal key deliberately differ so downstream readers (translate/server/copy) were untouched.
findings.detail: default_arg->positional_arg (validates the keyword's single positional formula argument, e.g. the 50 in ppPct(50)); copy_defaults->ui_text_defaults (ties to the public ui_text vocabulary). validate_default_arg was renamed to validate_positional_arg and its message; validate_copy_defaults kept its function name but its messages now say "ui_text_defaults". Any code or test reading entry$default_arg/entry$copy_defaults still works.
findings.verification: Re-verified by symbol at HEAD a58dadd. The only R/ behavioral drift since this finding's last verification is consumer_upstream_source_state's host-scope identity (ADR 0026 host env-rename clear); that hunk does not touch this finding's cited symbol(s), which are present and unchanged at HEAD. Claim re-confirmed.

findings.claim: The single-instance and panel-host source paths already SHARE resolve_upload_source (the panel-host path calls the same master impl), so the genuine remaining cross-file divergence is only the renderUI bodies + the seeding-decision closures/latches + the spec normalization — and those differ by reactive dependencies (single-instance is state-backed; the host runs state=NULL per ADR 0006), so a forced full collapse would couple them artificially.
findings.detail: There is NO apply_spec_at_boot_host twin in source — apply_spec_at_boot exists in server.R; the host spec handling is a separate small loop in shared-ui.R (~line 750). Refines the prior "two parallel source-handling paths" finding: the resolution CORE is deduped; the divergence is reactive-shape, not copy-paste.
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7. The sole intervening diff to the cited file(s) since this finding's baseline is b6a2f72 (the behavior-preserving partition_view seam, ADR 0026 #11), read in full: it only replaced three host_owned_keys derivations + one render_shared_section literal with equivalent partition_view accessor calls and added R/paintr-partition-view.R. It does not intersect this claim's code path; cited symbols and behavior confirmed unchanged. (Line numbers in R/paintr-app.R after ~1168 shifted +4 and in R/paintr-shared-ui.R after ~416 shifted +3; verify by symbol.)

findings.claim: The three source binding-name derivations are genuinely DIFFERENT per call site (not safely collapsible into one binding_name_for_source): try_bind_source_default splits lookup_name vs binding_name (post-PLAN-02 the empty-shortcut path looks up under the user name but BINDS under node$auto_name), feeds resolve_expr `node$default`; panel_owned_binding_name feeds resolve_expr the resolved `df`; try_bind_source_default_resolved takes the resolved shortcut and feeds resolve_expr `file_info`; auto-name fallback conditions differ per ADR 0024/0025/0023.
findings.detail: A unified derivor would be a shallow multi-flag pass-through and would risk behaviour change in the seeding-sensitive path. The only genuinely shared kernel across them is the syntactic-name validation predicate.
findings.verification: Re-verified by symbol at HEAD a58dadd. The only R/ behavioral drift since this finding's last verification is consumer_upstream_source_state's host-scope identity (ADR 0026 host env-rename clear); that hunk does not touch this finding's cited symbol(s), which are present and unchanged at HEAD. Claim re-confirmed.

findings.claim: The verb-derived param_override (`{param}` -> "verb()") and " in verb()" label_suffix for a pipeline-stage placeholder are now derived once by stage_overrides_for_entry(entry, drop_suffix); both the static build path and the renderUI re-derivation path call it.
findings.detail: drop_suffix = !is.na(entry$stage_id) at the render-time site (suffix suppressed when an enclosing stage-group header already names the verb). Post-PLAN-01 the panel-level args no longer reach invoke_build_ui, which is why the renderUI body must re-derive — that re-derivation was the duplication.
findings.verification: Confirmed stage_overrides_for_entry(entry,drop_suffix) (build-ui.R:282-293) returns param_override=verb() and label_suffix=' in verb()'; both build_pipeline_stage_ui (static) and pipeline_override_for_node (renderUI re-derive) call it.

findings.claim: On any commit that changes R/, the pre-commit hook regenerates and auto-stages dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/symbol-lines.json (it prints "[pre-commit] R/ changed — rebuilding symbol-lines.json"); use `git add -A` so the regenerated file is included.
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7. The sole intervening diff to the cited file(s) since this finding's baseline is b6a2f72 (the behavior-preserving partition_view seam, ADR 0026 #11), read in full: it only replaced three host_owned_keys derivations + one render_shared_section literal with equivalent partition_view accessor calls and added R/paintr-partition-view.R. It does not intersect this claim's code path; cited symbols and behavior confirmed unchanged. (Line numbers in R/paintr-app.R after ~1168 shifted +4 and in R/paintr-shared-ui.R after ~416 shifted +3; verify by symbol.)

findings.claim: An automated duplication/refactor sweep systematically OVER-states duplication — multiple ggpaintr candidates flagged as copy-paste were on inspection either per-site-different (source binding-name derivation), an already-shared single helper with N callers (collect_orphan_shared_stages, resolve_upload_source), or already-thin wrappers (ptr_app/ptr_app_grid); the discipline that held is to run the deletion test on each candidate and extract only the genuinely-identical kernel (e.g. the predicate, not the derivation).
findings.detail: Concrete refinements made: #5 scoped to param_override (orphan-stage left); #7 reduced to is_syntactic_name (derivation left); #2 left the column-vector normalizer separate; #8 preserved the layer/source key duality; #12 deep-left entirely.
findings.source: this session's candidate verification vs the explorer-synthesis catalog (ADR 0026)
findings.verification: Re-verified by symbol at HEAD a58dadd. The only R/ behavioral drift since this finding's last verification is consumer_upstream_source_state's host-scope identity (ADR 0026 host env-rename clear); that hunk does not touch this finding's cited symbol(s), which are present and unchanged at HEAD. Claim re-confirmed.
